steps.title: Identify the relationship between angles
steps.content: The problem states that triangles $JKL$ and $PQR$ are congruent, with $J$ corresponding to $P$. In congruent triangles, corresponding angles are equal. Therefore, $\angle P = \angle J$.

steps.title: Find angle P
steps.content: We're given that $\angle J = 45°$. Since $\angle P = \angle J$, we have $\angle P = 45°$.
